title: Activision Blizzard acquisition for $68.7 billion
decisionQuestion: Should Microsoft acquire Activision Blizzard for $95 per share in an all-cash transaction?
situation: Microsoft has agreed to acquire Activision Blizzard for $68.7 billion inclusive of net cash at $95 per share.
objectives: Expand gaming portfolio and content library
conditions: Both boards of directors have approved the transaction

options.expectedBenefit: Acquire Activision Blizzard's portfolio at $95 per share
options.principalRisk: Regulatory scrutiny of a large gaming merger

options.expectedBenefit: Avoid potential antitrust and regulatory challenges
options.principalRisk: Loss of strategic content and portfolio growth

observations: The transaction is valued at $68.7 billion inclusive of net cash
Both boards of directors have already approved the deal
inferences: Board approval suggests internal due diligence is complete
All-cash structure simplifies closing but concentrates financial outlay

evidenceSpans.exactQuote: Microsoft will acquire Activision Blizzard for $95.00 per share, in an all-cash transaction valued at $68.7 billion, inclusive of Activision Blizzard’s net cash.
The transaction has been approved by the boards of directors of both Microsoft and Activision Blizzard.
Shareholder and financial information, as well as today’s 6:00 a.m. Pacific time conference call with investors and analysts, is available at http://www.microsoft.com/en-us/investor.
We’ve agreed to acquire Activision Blizzard for $95 per share in an all-cash transaction valued at $68.7 billion, inclusive of Activision Blizzard’s net cash.
evidenceSpans.speaker: Amy Hood
